sections.paragraph:
unused in Kal. Arab. زَهِمَ to stink, to become rancid (when speaking of fat). Chald. to stink, to be filthy. This root is used in the Zabian, of water when it has a stinking smell. צָחַן, זָנַח, زنخ are kindred roots.

Piel, to regard as stinking or filthy, thus to regard with disgust, to loathe, or to be weary of. Job 33:20, זהֲמַתּוּ לֶ֑חֶם “he loathes it, namely bread.” The suffix is pleonastic; comp. Lehrg. § 195, 2.
